title: An afternoon at the bathhouse in Budapest
summary: A short story about a first visit to a public bath in a city famous for them.
topic: An afternoon at the bathhouse in Budapest
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about your first visit to a public bath, sauna, or hot spring abroad. Describe what you saw, what you did wrong, and how you felt by the end. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: I had never been to a public bath before that grey Thursday in Budapest…
I followed the wrong arrow twice and ended up in…
By the time I lowered myself into the warm outdoor pool…
sampleText: I had never been to a public bath before that grey Thursday in Budapest. A colleague at work told me, 'You cannot leave this city without going to Széchenyi at least once.' So I bought my ticket on a slow afternoon and tried to look like I knew what I was doing. I did not. I followed the wrong arrow twice and ended up in the women's changing room before a polite cleaner pointed me back to the men's side. While I was looking for my locker, an older man in nothing but slippers was laughing at me kindly. The tiled rooms smelled of warm stone and very faintly of eggs. I lowered myself into a small indoor pool first, then walked through a corridor of steam, and finally stepped out into the wide warm outdoor pool under a cold grey sky. Snow had started to fall, and the steam was rising around the chess players who were playing in the water at the edge. I floated on my back for forty minutes. By the time I finished, my fingers were wrinkled, my mind was completely empty, and I had finally understood what my colleague meant. Some places you must enter wrong before you can enter them right.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for 'never... before that day'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I had never been to a public bath before that grey Thursday' dùng past perfect ('had been') + 'never' + 'before + thời điểm quá khứ' để chỉ kinh nghiệm không có cho tới một thời điểm quá khứ cụ thể.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I had never been to a public bath before that grey Thursday' uses past perfect ('had been') + 'never' + 'before + past time' to mark an experience absent up to a specific past moment.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for sustained background
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'the steam was rising around the chess players who were playing in the water' — hai past continuous chồng lên nhau tả khung cảnh dài đang diễn ra.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'The steam was rising around the chess players who were playing in the water' — overlapping past continuous depicts an extended, ongoing scene.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi chưa bao giờ đi nhà tắm công cộng trước cái thứ Năm xám ấy ở Budapest. Một đồng nghiệp ở chỗ làm bảo tôi, 'Bạn không thể rời thành phố này mà chưa đến Széchenyi ít nhất một lần.' Vậy nên tôi mua vé vào một buổi chiều thong thả và cố tỏ ra như tôi biết mình đang làm gì. Tôi không biết. Tôi đi theo mũi tên sai hai lần và đã đến phòng thay đồ nữ trước khi một người dọn dẹp lịch sự chỉ tôi quay lại bên nam. Trong khi tôi đang tìm tủ khóa của mình, một ông cụ chỉ đi dép lê đang cười với tôi hiền lành. Các phòng lát gạch thơm mùi đá ấm và rất thoang thoảng mùi trứng. Tôi từ từ thả mình vào một hồ nhỏ trong nhà trước, rồi đi qua một hành lang hơi nước, và cuối cùng bước ra hồ lớn ấm ngoài trời dưới bầu trời xám lạnh. Tuyết đã bắt đầu rơi, và hơi nước đang bốc lên quanh những người chơi cờ đang chơi trong nước ở rìa hồ. Tôi nổi ngửa trên lưng trong bốn mươi phút. Đến khi tôi xong, các ngón tay của tôi nhăn nheo, đầu óc tôi hoàn toàn trống rỗng, và tôi cuối cùng đã hiểu ý đồng nghiệp tôi muốn nói gì. Có những nơi bạn phải bước vào sai trước khi bạn có thể bước vào đúng.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past perfect opening.
options: I had never been to a public bath before that grey Thursday in Budapest.
I have never been to a public bath before that grey Thursday in Budapest.
I was never been to a public bath before that grey Thursday in Budapest.
I had never being to a public bath before that grey Thursday in Budapest.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had + never + been' for absence of experience up to a past time.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence with 'cannot + base verb'.
options: You cannot to leave this city without going to Széchenyi at least once.
You cannot leave this city without going to Széchenyi at least once.
You cannot leaving this city without going to Széchenyi at least once.
You cannot left this city without going to Széchenyi at least once.
a private bedroom
the inside of a small swimming pool
a public building where people go to wash and relax in warm pools
a hospital for skin problems
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Bathhouse' = a public facility for bathing and relaxation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct 'while' + past continuous sentence.
options: While I looking for my locker, an older man was laughing at me kindly.
While I look for my locker, an older man was laughing at me kindly.
While I am looking for my locker, an older man was laughing at me kindly.
While I was looking for my locker, an older man was laughing at me kindly.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous in both clauses for parallel ongoing actions.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence with 'smell of'.
options: The tiled rooms smelled of warm stone.
The tiled rooms smell of warm stone.
The tiled rooms smelled at warm stone.
The tiled rooms smelled to warm stone.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'smelled' + 'of + noun' is the standard collocation.

options: to make oneself less important
to slowly move yourself down into a place
to laugh quietly at oneself
to climb up stairs
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Lower oneself into' = to slowly bring oneself down into water, a chair, etc.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past perfect sentence about the snow.
options: Snow have started to fall.
Snow was started to fall.
Snow had started to fall.
Snow had start to fall.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had started' for an event that began before another past moment.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural sentence about floating.
options: I floated in my back for forty minutes.
I float on my back for forty minutes.
I am floated on my back for forty minutes.
I floated on my back for forty minutes.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'floated' + 'on (one's) back' is the fixed preposition.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past perfect realisation sentence.
options: I had finally understood what my colleague meant.
I have finally understood what my colleague meant.
I was finally understood what my colleague meant.
I had finally understand what my colleague meant.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had + understood' for a realisation completed by a past moment.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence with reduced relative clause.
options: the chess players who playing in the water at the edge
the chess players who were playing in the water at the edge
the chess players were playing in the water at the edge
the chess players who play in the water at the edge

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural closing line.
options: Some places you must enter wrong before you can enter it right.
Some places you must to enter wrong before you can enter them right.
Some places you must enter wrong before you can enter them right.
Some places you must entered wrong before you can enter them right.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do some experiences only make sense after you have done them wrong first? Is there value in getting things wrong on purpose, just to learn how they really work?
